title: Wild Molten Ethiopian Chicken Fir Fir
description: A fiery Ethiopian-inspired fir fir that combines double-fried chicken with a peppery dark chocolate sauce, served molten in an earthenware bowl.
instructions: Pat the chicken pieces dry and season with salt and smoked paprika.
Lightly coat each piece with chickpea flour, shaking off excess.
Heat oil in a shallow pan to 165°C and fry the chicken until golden, about 4 minutes.
Remove the chicken, drain on paper towels, then increase heat to 190°C and re-fry for another 2 minutes until extra crisp.
In a small saucepan, melt the dark chocolate over low heat with coconut milk, stirring until smooth.
Add berbere spice, lime juice, ginger, garlic powder, and sliced chili to the chocolate mixture and stir until blended.
Return the double-fried chicken to the pan, tossing to coat evenly in the molten sauce.
Transfer the chicken and sauce into an earthenware bowl, pouring any excess sauce over the top.
Sprinkle crushed roasted peanuts on the surface for crunch and extra flavor.
Serve immediately while the sauce remains molten and the chicken stays crispy.
